title: అమ్మ మనసు, ధర్మ మార్గం
moral: జీవితంలో కష్టాలు వచ్చినా, సరైన మార్గంలో నిలబడటం, విశ్వాసం ఉంచుకోవడం నిజమైన బలం.
main_character: Kausalya: A queen of Ayodhya, mother of Lord Rama, known for her deep devotion and maternal strength. She has a serene countenance, often clad in soft silks, and her eyes reflect profound wisdom and compassion. Always the same character across every scene.
setting: The royal palace in Ayodhya, a grand structure of white marble and intricate carvings, surrounded by lush gardens and a bustling city. Sunlight streams through arched windows onto polished floors, illuminating vibrant tapestries. Same location across every scene.

scenes.text: అయోధ్య రాజప్రాసాదంలో, కౌసల్యాదేవి తన ఉదయం పూజలో లీనమై ఉంది. ఆమె కళ్ళు మూసుకుని, ముఖం ప్రశాంతంగా, పెదవులపై మంత్రాలు మెల్లగా కదిలాయి. గది నిండా జాస్మిన్, ధూపపు సువాసనలు నిండిపోయాయి, ఆమె శ్వాసతో కలిసిపోయాయి. ఆమె చేతులు నమస్కార ముద్రలో నిశ్చలంగా ఉన్నాయి, ప్రతి కదలికలోనూ అంకితభావం కనిపించింది. "ఓ దేవదేవా, నా రామునికి శుభం కలుగుగాక," అని ఆమె మనసులోనే ప్రార్థించింది. పూజ ముగించి కళ్ళు తెరవగానే — ఆమె ఎదురుగా ప్రియపుత్రుడు రాముడు నిలబడి ఉన్నాడు. ఆమె పెదవులపై చిరునవ్వు మెరిసింది.
scenes.image_prompt: The woman opens her eyes after prayer to see her son standing before her. A gentle smile lights up her lips as she gazes at him. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Rama: a young prince with dark blue-black (shyama) complexion, tall and broad-shouldered, long arms reaching to his knees (Ajanabahu). During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot (jata-mukuta), carrying his curved bow Kodanda and a quiver of arrows over his shoulder. Lotus-shaped eyes with a gentle reddish tinge at the corners. Noble, calm expression. Soft, golden pre-dawn light filtering through an open archway of the puja room, illuminating the offerings.
scenes.scene_visual: The woman opens her eyes after prayer to see her son standing before her. A gentle smile lights up her lips as she gazes at him.
scenes.text_te_en: Ayodhya Raajapraasaadanlo, Kausalyaadevi Tana Udayam Pujalo Linamai Undi. Aame Kallu Musukuni, Mukham Prashaantangaa, Pedavulapai Mantraalu Mellagaa Kadilaayi. Gadi Nindaa Jaasmin, Dhupapu Suvaasanalu Nindipoyaayi, Aame Shvaasato Kalisipoyaayi. Aame Chetulu Namaskaara Mudralo Nishchalangaa Unnaayi, Prati Kadalikalonu Ankitabhaavam Kanipinchindi. "O Devadevaa, Naa Raamuniki Shubham Kalugugaaka," Ani Aame Manasulone Praarthinchindi. Puja Muginchi Kallu Teravagaane — Aame Edurugaa Priyaputrudu Raamudu Nilabadi Unnaadu. Aame Pedavulapai Chirunavvu Merisindi.
scenes.text_en: In the royal palace of Ayodhya, Kausalya Devi was deeply engrossed in her morning prayers. Her eyes were closed, her face peaceful, and mantras gently moved on her lips. The room was filled with the fragrance of jasmine and incense, blending with her breath. Her hands were still in a prayer pose, and devotion was visible in every movement. "Oh Lord of Lords, may good fortune befall my Rama," she prayed silently in her heart. As she finished her prayers and opened her eyes — her beloved son Rama stood before her. A gentle smile lit up her lips.

scenes.text: రాముడు రాకతో కౌసల్య ముఖం వెలిగిపోయింది, ఆ క్షణం నుండి రాజప్రాసాదం సందడితో నిండిపోయింది. రాముని పట్టాభిషేక వార్త విన్నప్పుడు, ఆమె ముఖం నిండా సంతోషం చిప్పిల్లింది, ప్రతి కదలికలోనూ ఆనందం ఉట్టిపడింది. శంఖారావాలు, బూరల శబ్దాలు ప్రాసాదమంతా ప్రతిధ్వనించాయి, ఆమె గుండె ఆనందంతో ఉప్పొంగింది. "నా రాముడు పట్టాభిషిక్తుడు కాబోతున్నాడు!" అని ఆమె మనసులో పదే పదే అనుకుంది. ఆమె కళ్ళు మెరిశాయి, గర్వంతో తల ఎత్తుకుంది, చేతులు అనాయాసంగా కలిశాయి. ఇంతలో — కోలాహలం ఒక్కసారిగా ఆగిపోయింది. ప్రాసాదమంతా నిశ్శబ్దం అలుముకుంది, అందరి కళ్ళలో ఆశ్చర్యం.
scenes.image_prompt: The bustling palace suddenly falls silent, and all activity ceases. A look of surprise spreads across the faces of everyone present. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Rama: a young prince with dark blue-black (shyama) complexion, tall and broad-shouldered, long arms reaching to his knees (Ajanabahu). During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot (jata-mukuta), carrying his curved bow Kodanda and a quiver of arrows over his shoulder. Lotus-shaped eyes with a gentle reddish tinge at the corners. Noble, calm expression. Bright, celebratory midday sun reflecting off the white marble palace walls, casting sharp shadows.
scenes.scene_visual: The bustling palace suddenly falls silent, and all activity ceases. A look of surprise spreads across the faces of everyone present.
scenes.text_te_en: Raamudu Raakato Kausalya Mukham Veligipoyindi, Aa Kshanam Nundi Raajapraasaadam Sandadito Nindipoyindi. Raamuni Pattaabhisheka Vaarta Vinnappudu, Aame Mukham Nindaa Santosham Chippillindi, Prati Kadalikalonu Aanandam Uttipadindi. Shankhaaraavaalu, Burala Shabdaalu Praasaadamantaa Pratidhvaninchaayi, Aame Gunde Aanandanto Uppongindi. "Naa Raamudu Pattaabhishiktudu Kaabotunnaadu!" Ani Aame Manasulo Pade Pade Anukundi. Aame Kallu Merishaayi, Garvanto Tala Ettukundi, Chetulu Anaayaasangaa Kalishaayi. Intalo — Kolaahalam Okkasaarigaa Aagipoyindi. Praasaadamantaa Nishshabdam Alumukundi, Andari Kallalo Aashcharyan.
scenes.text_en: With Rama's arrival, Kausalya's face lit up, and from that moment, the royal palace was filled with excitement. When she heard the news of Rama's coronation, her face overflowed with joy, and happiness radiated from her every movement. The sounds of conch shells and trumpets echoed throughout the palace, and her heart swelled with delight. "My Rama is about to be crowned!" she thought repeatedly in her mind. Her eyes sparkled, she held her head high with pride, and her hands effortlessly joined in prayer. Suddenly — the commotion stopped. Silence fell over the entire palace, and surprise was in everyone's eyes.

scenes.text: ఆ నిశ్శబ్దం మధ్యలోనే, దశరథ మహారాజు కౌసల్య వద్దకు అడుగులు వేశాడు, అతని భుజాలు కుంగిపోయాయి. అతని గొంతు, ఎప్పుడూ గంభీరంగా ఉండేది, ఇప్పుడు వణుకుతున్న గుసగుసగా మారింది. కౌసల్య గుండె ఒక్కసారిగా ఆగిపోయినట్లైంది, ఆమెకు మాట రాలేదు. "రామా, నీవు అరణ్యమునకు వెళ్ళవలసి ఉన్నది," అని రాజు విలవిలలాడుతూ పలికాడు. ఆమె చేతులు నోటికి అడ్డుపెట్టుకుంది, కళ్ళలో నీళ్ళు సుడులు తిరిగాయి, శరీరం ఒక్కసారిగా బిగుసుకుపోయింది. ఈ మాటలను ఆమె నమ్మలేకపోయింది — ఇది నిజమా? ఆమె చూపులు రాముని వైపు పడ్డాయి, గుండె ముక్కలవుతున్న బాధ.
scenes.image_prompt: The woman brings her hands to her mouth in shock, her body tensing. Tears well up in her eyes as she struggles to comprehend the words. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Rama: a young prince with dark blue-black (shyama) complexion, tall and broad-shouldered, long arms reaching to his knees (Ajanabahu). During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot (jata-mukuta), carrying his curved bow Kodanda and a quiver of arrows over his shoulder. Lotus-shaped eyes with a gentle reddish tinge at the corners. Noble, calm expression. The joyous midday light now feels harsh, casting long, unsettling shadows as the atmosphere darkens with sorrow.
scenes.scene_visual: The woman brings her hands to her mouth in shock, her body tensing. Tears well up in her eyes as she struggles to comprehend the words.
scenes.text_te_en: Aa Nishshabdam Madhyalone, Dasharatha Mahaaraaju Kausalya Vaddaku Adugulu Veshaadu, Atani Bhujaalu Kungipoyaayi. Atani Gontu, Eppudu Gambhirangaa Undedi, Ippudu Vanukutunna Gusagusagaa Maarindi. Kausalya Gunde Okkasaarigaa Aagipoyinatlaindi, Aameku Maata Raaledu. "Raamaa, Nivu Aranyamunaku Vellavalasi Unnadi," Ani Raaju Vilavilalaadutu Palikaadu. Aame Chetulu Notiki Addupettukundi, Kallalo Nillu Sudulu Tirigaayi, Shariram Okkasaarigaa Bigusukupoyindi. I Maatalanu Aame Nammalekapoyindi — Idi Nijamaa? Aame Chupulu Raamuni Vaipu Paddaayi, Gunde Mukkalavutunna Baadha.
scenes.text_en: Amidst that silence, King Dasharatha walked towards Kausalya, his shoulders slumped. His voice, usually majestic, now became a trembling whisper. Kausalya's heart felt like it stopped, and she couldn't speak. "Rama, you must go to the forest," the king said, trembling. She covered her mouth with her hands, tears welled up in her eyes, and her body suddenly tensed. She couldn't believe these words — was this real? Her gaze fell upon Rama, her heart breaking with pain.

scenes.text: రాముని అరణ్యవాసపు మాటలు విని కౌసల్య గుండె తరుక్కుపోయింది, ఆమె ముఖం పాలిపోయింది. ఆ క్షణంలో ఆమెకు రామునితో విడిపోవడం అసాధ్యంగా అనిపించింది. ఆమె హడావిడిగా కదిలింది, ఆమె పట్టుకున్న పట్టుచీర సవ్వడి గదిలో వినిపించింది. కళ్ళ నుండి కన్నీరు ధారలుగా కారుతూనే ఉన్నాయి, ఆమె చిన్న మూటను గట్టిగా పట్టుకుంది. "నన్ను నీతో అరణ్యమునకు తీసుకువెళ్ళు, నాన్నా!" అని దీనంగా పలికింది. కానీ రాముడు ఆమె చేతులను మెల్లగా పట్టుకున్నాడు, అతని కళ్ళలో బాధ స్పష్టంగా కనిపించింది. ఆమెకు ఆ క్షణం ప్రపంచం ఆగిపోయినట్లు అనిపించింది.
scenes.image_prompt: The woman desperately clutches a small bundle, pleading with her son. Her son gently holds her hands, his eyes reflecting clear pain. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Rama: a young prince with dark blue-black (shyama) complexion, tall and broad-shouldered, long arms reaching to his knees (Ajanabahu). During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot (jata-mukuta), carrying his curved bow Kodanda and a quiver of arrows over his shoulder. Lotus-shaped eyes with a gentle reddish tinge at the corners. Noble, calm expression. A dim, interior palace corridor, lit by a single oil lamp, creating a somber, intimate glow around Kausalya and Rama.
scenes.scene_visual: The woman desperately clutches a small bundle, pleading with her son. Her son gently holds her hands, his eyes reflecting clear pain.
scenes.text_te_en: Raamuni Aranyavaasapu Maatalu Vini Kausalya Gunde Tarukkupoyindi, Aame Mukham Paalipoyindi. Aa Kshananlo Aameku Raamunito Vidipovadam Asaadhyangaa Anipinchindi. Aame Hadaavidigaa Kadilindi, Aame Pattukunna Pattuchira Savvadi Gadilo Vinipinchindi. Kalla Nundi Kanniru Dhaaralugaa Kaarutune Unnaayi, Aame Chinna Mutanu Gattigaa Pattukundi. "Nannu Nito Aranyamunaku Tisukuvellu, Naannaa!" Ani Dinangaa Palikindi. Kaani Raamudu Aame Chetulanu Mellagaa Pattukunnaadu, Atani Kallalo Baadha Spashtangaa Kanipinchindi. Aameku Aa Kshanam Prapancham Aagipoyinatlu Anipinchindi.
scenes.text_en: Hearing about Rama's exile to the forest, Kausalya's heart was torn, and her face turned pale. At that moment, parting with Rama seemed impossible to her. She moved hurriedly, the rustle of her silk saree audible in the room. Tears flowed continuously from her eyes, and she clutched a small bundle tightly. "Take me with you to the forest, my son!" she pleaded. But Rama gently held her hands, and the pain in his eyes was clear. To her, the world seemed to stop at that moment.

scenes.text: రాముడు తన ధర్మనిష్టను, తండ్రి మాటను నిలబెట్టుకోవాల్సిన ఆవశ్యకతను శాంతంగా వివరించాడు. కౌసల్య మనసులో అల్లకల్లోలం రేగింది, తల్లి ప్రేమ, ధర్మం మధ్య తటపటాయించింది. ఆమె గుండె చప్పుడు ఆమెకే స్పష్టంగా వినిపించింది, ప్రతి అక్షరంలోనూ ధైర్యం నింపుకుంది. ఆమె కళ్ళ నుండి ఒకే ఒక్క కన్నీటి బిందువు జారింది, కానీ ఆమె చూపు స్థిరంగా, దృఢంగా మారింది. "ధర్మమును రక్షించు, రామా! నీకు శుభం కలుగుగాక," అని ఆమె నిబ్బరంగా పలికింది. ఆ క్షణంలో — ఆమెకు అన్నీ అర్థమయ్యాయి. కొత్త ప్రశాంతతతో ఆమె సీత, లక్ష్మణుల వైపు తిరిగింది.
scenes.image_prompt: A single tear falls from the woman's eye, but her gaze becomes steady and firm. She speaks with composure, her expression now resolute. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Rama: a young prince with dark blue-black (shyama) complexion, tall and broad-shouldered, long arms reaching to his knees (Ajanabahu). During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot (jata-mukuta), carrying his curved bow Kodanda and a quiver of arrows over his shoulder. Lotus-shaped eyes with a gentle reddish tinge at the corners. Noble, calm expression. The soft, diffused light from a nearby window illuminates Rama's calm face and Kausalya's tear-streaked but resolute expression, a sense of sacredness in the moment.
scenes.scene_visual: A single tear falls from the woman's eye, but her gaze becomes steady and firm. She speaks with composure, her expression now resolute.
scenes.text_te_en: Raamudu Tana Dharmanishtanu, Tandri Maatanu Nilabettukovaalsina Aavashyakatanu Shaantangaa Vivarinchaadu. Kausalya Manasulo Allakallolam Regindi, Talli Prema, Dharmam Madhya Tatapataayinchindi. Aame Gunde Chappudu Aameke Spashtangaa Vinipinchindi, Prati Aksharanlonu Dhairyam Nimpukundi. Aame Kalla Nundi Oke Okka Kanniti Binduvu Jaarindi, Kaani Aame Chupu Sthirangaa, Drridhangaa Maarindi. "Dharmamunu Rakshinchu, Raamaa! Niku Shubham Kalugugaaka," Ani Aame Nibbarangaa Palikindi. Aa Kshananlo — Aameku Anni Arthamayyaayi. Kotta Prashaantatato Aame Sita, Lakshmanula Vaipu Tirigindi.
scenes.text_en: Rama calmly explained his devotion to duty (dharma) and the necessity of upholding his father's word. Kausalya's mind was in turmoil, wavering between a mother's love and duty. She could clearly hear her own heartbeat, and with every word, she filled herself with courage. Only a single tear fell from her eyes, but her gaze became steady and firm. "Protect dharma, Rama! May good fortune befall you," she said with composure. At that moment — she understood everything. With new serenity, she turned towards Sita and Lakshmana.

scenes.text: ధైర్యంగా నిలబడి, కౌసల్య రాముని వీడ్కోలు పలికింది, ఆమె గొంతులో వణుకు లేదు. లక్ష్మణుడు, సీత కూడా ఆమె పాదాలకు నమస్కరించారు, ఆమె వారిని ఆప్యాయంగా చూసింది. "తల్లిగా నా దీవెనలు ఎల్లప్పుడూ మీ వెంట ఉంటాయి," అని ఆమె దృఢంగా పలికింది. రాముని చెంపపై ఆమె చేయి మెల్లగా తాకింది — అది నిశ్శబ్ద ఆశీర్వాదం. ఆమె కళ్ళలో నీళ్ళు నిండినా, అవి కింద పడకుండా ఆపుకుంది, పెదవులపై చిన్న నవ్వు మెరిసింది. ఆమె అచంచలమైన విశ్వాసం ఆమె ముఖంలో స్పష్టంగా కనిపించింది. ప్రాసాదపు ద్వారం వద్ద నిలబడి, వారి నిష్క్రమణను చూస్తూ — ఆమె ఒంటరిగా మిగిలిపోయింది.
scenes.image_prompt: The woman gently touches her son's cheek, offering a silent blessing. Though her eyes are filled with tears, she holds them back, a small smile gracing her lips. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Rama: a young prince with dark blue-black (shyama) complexion, tall and broad-shouldered, long arms reaching to his knees (Ajanabahu). During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot (jata-mukuta), carrying his curved bow Kodanda and a quiver of arrows over his shoulder. Lotus-shaped eyes with a gentle reddish tinge at the corners. Noble, calm expression. The golden light of late afternoon, casting long shadows as the sun begins to set, symbolizing the end of an era and the beginning of a long journey.
scenes.scene_visual: The woman gently touches her son's cheek, offering a silent blessing. Though her eyes are filled with tears, she holds them back, a small smile gracing her lips.
scenes.text_te_en: Dhairyangaa Nilabadi, Kausalya Raamuni Vidkolu Palikindi, Aame Gontulo Vanuku Ledu. Lakshmanudu, Sita Kudaa Aame Paadaalaku Namaskarinchaaru, Aame Vaarini Aapyaayangaa Chusindi. "Talligaa Naa Divenalu Ellappudu Mi Venta Untaayi," Ani Aame Drridhangaa Palikindi. Raamuni Chempapai Aame Cheyi Mellagaa Taakindi — Adi Nishshabda Aashirvaadan. Aame Kallalo Nillu Nindinaa, Avi Kinda Padakundaa Aapukundi, Pedavulapai Chinna Navvu Merisindi. Aame Achanchalamaina Vishvaasam Aame Mukhanlo Spashtangaa Kanipinchindi. Praasaadapu Dvaaram Vadda Nilabadi, Vaari Nishkramananu Chustu — Aame Ontarigaa Migilipoyindi.
scenes.text_en: Standing bravely, Kausalya bid farewell to Rama, her voice unwavering. Lakshmana and Sita also bowed at her feet, and she looked at them affectionately. "As a mother, my blessings will always be with you," she said firmly. Her hand gently touched Rama's cheek — it was a silent blessing. Though her eyes were filled with tears, she held them back from falling, and a small smile appeared on her lips. Her unwavering faith was clearly visible on her face. Standing at the palace gate, watching their departure — she was left alone.

scenes.text: సంవత్సరాలు గడిచిపోయాయి, కానీ కౌసల్య దినచర్య మారలేదు, ఆమె ముఖంలో ప్రశాంతత నిలిచింది. రాజధర్మాన్ని నిర్వర్తిస్తూనే, ఆమె రోజువారీ పూజలను భక్తిశ్రద్ధలతో కొనసాగించింది. కిటికీ బయట ఆకుల మెల్లని సవ్వడి కాలం గడుస్తున్నదని చెప్పింది, కానీ ఆమె ఆశ మాత్రం చెక్కుచెదరలేదు. రాముని చిత్రపటం వైపు చూస్తూ, ఆమె పెదవులపై ఒక మృదువైన చిరునవ్వు కనిపించింది. "నా ధర్మము, నా విశ్వాసము ఎప్పటికీ చెక్కుచెదరవు," అని ఆమె మనసులో దృఢంగా అనుకుంది. ఆమె మనసులో శాంతి, రాముని రాక కోసం ఎదురుచూపు — అది అయోధ్య ప్రజలందరికీ ధైర్యం. ఆమె ధైర్యం అయోధ్యకు ఒక మార్గదర్శిగా నిలిచింది.
scenes.image_prompt: The woman gazes at a picture of her son, a soft smile appearing on her lips. Her face reflects a deep sense of peace and unwavering hope. Gentle dusk light, warm amber, filling her private chamber, creating a peaceful and reflective atmosphere.
scenes.scene_visual: The woman gazes at a picture of her son, a soft smile appearing on her lips. Her face reflects a deep sense of peace and unwavering hope.
scenes.text_te_en: Sanvatsaraalu Gadichipoyaayi, Kaani Kausalya Dinacharya Maaraledu, Aame Mukhanlo Prashaantata Nilichindi. Raajadharmaanni Nirvartistune, Aame Rojuvaari Pujalanu Bhaktishraddhalato Konasaaginchindi. Kitiki Bayata Aakula Mellani Savvadi Kaalam Gadustunnadani Cheppindi, Kaani Aame Aasha Maatram Chekkuchedaraledu. Raamuni Chitrapatam Vaipu Chustu, Aame Pedavulapai Oka Mrriduvaina Chirunavvu Kanipinchindi. "Naa Dharmamu, Naa Vishvaasamu Eppatiki Chekkuchedaravu," Ani Aame Manasulo Drridhangaa Anukundi. Aame Manasulo Shaanti, Raamuni Raaka Kosam Eduruchupu — Adi Ayodhya Prajalandariki Dhairyan. Aame Dhairyam Ayodhyaku Oka Maargadarshigaa Nilichindi.
scenes.text_en: Years passed, but Kausalya's daily routine remained unchanged, and peace remained on her face. While fulfilling her royal duties, she continued her daily prayers with devotion. The gentle rustle of leaves outside the window told of time passing, but her hope remained unshaken. Looking at Rama's portrait, a soft smile appeared on her lips. "My duty and my faith will never waver," she firmly thought in her mind. The peace in her heart and her wait for Rama's return — it was courage for all the people of Ayodhya. Her courage stood as a guide for Ayodhya.

supporting_characters.description: Rama: a young prince with dark blue-black (shyama) complexion, tall and broad-shouldered, long arms reaching to his knees (Ajanabahu). During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot (jata-mukuta), carrying his curved bow Kodanda and a quiver of arrows over his shoulder. Lotus-shaped eyes with a gentle reddish tinge at the corners. Noble, calm expression. Always the same character across every scene.

Lakshmana: a young prince (mid-twenties) with a warm golden-brown complexion, muscular and alert — slightly shorter and more compact than Rama. During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot, carrying his bow and full quiver, always standing in a protective, watchful posture. He is NOT old, NOT a monk, NOT grey-haired — he is a vigorous young warrior-prince. Determined, loyal expression; the devoted younger brother. Always the same character across every scene.

Sita: a graceful young woman (mid-twenties) with a fair golden complexion and long dark hair adorned with jasmine flowers. Wearing a silk saree in deep red or golden hues, with traditional gold ornaments: necklace, bangles, nose ring, and earrings. Eyes reflecting deep wisdom and quiet dignity. Serene, composed expression. Always the same character across every scene.

Dasharatha: The aging King of Ayodhya, a noble and just ruler, clad in royal garments of deep blue and gold. His face, usually commanding, now carries the heavy lines of sorrow and regret. Always the same character across every scene.

quality_scores.notes: The story presents a strong narrative arc with a profound moral, showcasing Kausalya's internal struggle and ultimate strength, and the grammar is largely correct. However, the formal, descriptive prose lacks the warmth and conversational tone expected from a grandmother telling a bedtime story. The minimal, functional dialogue makes the narrative feel more like a report than an engaging tale. To significantly improve, the single most important fix is to infuse the narrative with more natural, character-specific dialogue and adopt a simpler, more oral storytelling style, perhaps with direct addresses to the child listener. This would enhance both authenticity and engagement.

moral_te_en: Jivitanlo Kashtaalu Vachchinaa, Saraina Maarganlo Nilabadatan, Vishvaasam Unchukovadam Nijamaina Balan.
title_en: A Mother's Heart, The Righteous Path
moral_en: Even when difficulties come in life, standing on the right path and having faith is true strength.
topic: కౌసల్య ధర్మ నిష్ట - కష్టాలలోనూ స్థైర్యం, మాతృత్వపు గొప్పదనం.
